Per-tube assessment (top to bottom: C, A, B, C, A, B)

Tube 1 (C, top):
- Color/transparency: pale yellow; liquid mostly clear to semi-transparent
- Clots/structures: single, well-defined circular yellow coagulum at the rounded tip; no visible strands
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot (compact localized clot with surrounding liquid)
- Density/homogeneity: clot appears dense and homogeneous; surrounding plasma uniform with fine microbubbles

Tube 2 (A):
- Color/transparency: pale yellow; semi-transparent
- Clots/structures: diffuse, irregular, cloud-like aggregate; edges indistinct; strand-like appearance
- Coagulation stage: early_fibrin
- Density/homogeneity: low-density, heterogeneous, wispy area; remainder of plasma uniform

Tube 3 (B):
- Color/transparency: pale yellow; semi-transparent
- Clots/structures: compact round nodule adhered near the tip; smaller than Tube 1 but sharply defined
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot
- Density/homogeneity: medium-to-high density, fairly homogeneous clot; surrounding liquid uniform

Tube 4 (C):
- Color/transparency: pale yellow; transparent to semi-transparent
- Clots/structures: no discrete clot or fibrin strands visible
- Coagulation stage: none
- Density/homogeneity: liquid appears uniform; no focal densities

Tube 5 (A):
- Color/transparency: pale yellow; semi-transparent
- Clots/structures: small, compact round clot; localized
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot (small)
- Density/homogeneity: moderate density, homogeneous clot; rest uniform

Tube 6 (B, bottom):
- Color/transparency: pale yellow; semi-transparent
- Clots/structures: prominent, compact round yellow coagulum at the tip; well-defined
- Coagulation stage: partial_clot (largest/most developed among the six)
- Density/homogeneity: high density, homogeneous clot; surrounding liquid uniform

Comparison
- Most advanced coagulation: Tube 6 (B, bottom), closely followed by Tube 1 (C, top)
- Least coagulation: Tube 4 (C, second from bottom) — no visible coagulum
- Visible differences: presence/absence of a clot; clot size (none → small → large), edge definition (wispy vs sharply bounded), and compactness/density vary between tubes. A shows one wispy and one small compact clot; B shows consistent, dense compact clots; C ranges from a dense compact clot to no visible clot.
- Overall visual difference: pronounced

openai.raw:
Observations per tube (A–C from left to right):
- All three show pale‑yellow plasma. Each tube contains a rounded central coagulum within otherwise clear plasma.
- No tube shows complete, tube‑filling coagulation or signs of lysis.

Tube A:
- Color/transparency: pale yellow; surrounding plasma clear.
- Structures: distinct central coagulum with visible internal fibrin strands crossing the core.
- Coagulation stage: partial clot (localized clot present; not filling tube).
- Density/homogeneity: medium density; inhomogeneous because of visible strands and internal patterning.

Tube B:
- Color/transparency: pale yellow; surrounding plasma clear.
- Structures: central coagulum is more compact, smoother, and more opaque; few/no visible strands.
- Coagulation stage: partial clot (appears more consolidated than A).
- Density/homogeneity: higher density; relatively homogeneous.

Tube C:
- Color/transparency: pale yellow; surrounding plasma clear.
- Structures: central coagulum present but more translucent with softer/less-defined edges; faint internal texture.
- Coagulation stage: early_fibrin to early partial clot (less consolidated than A and B).
- Density/homogeneity: lower density; heterogeneous, looser gel-like appearance.

Comparison:
- Most advanced coagulation: Tube B (most compact and opaque clot).
- Least advanced: Tube C (most translucent, least consolidated).
- Differences: B shows the most consolidated, opaque clot; A intermediate with evident fibrin strands; C shows the loosest, most translucent coagulum. Surrounding plasma looks similarly clear in all tubes.
- Overall visual difference: moderate.
